name: India

visa_free.notes: Citizens of Nepal visiting India are exempt from passport and visa requirements when travelling directly from Nepal by land or air. Any government-approved identity document with photo is accepted. No stay limit specified. However, Nepal citizens must carry a valid passport when entering India from China, Macau, Hong Kong, or Pakistan.

Citizens of Bhutan visiting India are exempt from passport and visa requirements when travelling directly from Bhutan by land or air. Any government-approved identity document with photo is accepted. No stay limit specified. However, Bhutan citizens must carry a valid passport (and visa for India) when entering India from China, Macau, Hong Kong, or Pakistan.

Visa-free entry for Maldivian nationals for Business, Medical treatment, or Tourism purposes only. The 90-day period applies to the stay in India. Must be in possession of a valid Passport/Travel Document.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on Arrival valid for 60 days with double-entry. Permitted purposes: Business, Tourism, Conference, Medical. Non-extendable and non-convertible. Available at 6 airports: Bengaluru, Chennai, Delhi, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Mumbai. Not available if applicant or parents/grandparents were born in or were permanent residents of Pakistan. Not available to holders of Diplomatic/Official passports. Fee: INR 2,000 or equivalent in foreign currency.

Visa on Arrival valid for 60 days with double-entry. Permitted purposes: Business, Tourism, Conference, Medical. Non-extendable and non-convertible. Available at 6 airports: Bengaluru, Chennai, Delhi, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Mumbai. Not available if applicant or parents/grandparents were born in or were permanent residents of Pakistan. Not available to holders of Diplomatic/Official passports. Fee: INR 2,000 or equivalent in foreign currency.

e_visa.notes: Eligible for India e-Visa. e-Tourist Visa available in 30-day (double entry), 1-year (multiple entry, max 180 days/calendar year), and 5-year (multiple entry, max 180 days/calendar year) variants. Other e-Visa types also available.
Eligible for India e-Visa. Listed as 'Cameroon Union Republic' on the official Indian government e-visa page.
Eligible for India e-Visa. Japan nationals also have a Visa on Arrival option (60 days, double entry) at 6 designated airports.
Eligible for India e-Visa. Listed as 'Macedonia' on the official Indian government e-visa page (officially 'North Macedonia' per UN, but India's official list retains 'Macedonia').
Eligible for India e-Visa. Korean nationals also have a Visa on Arrival option (60 days, double entry) at 6 designated airports.
Eligible for India e-Visa. Listed as 'Taiwan' on the official Indian government e-visa page.
Eligible for India e-Visa. UAE nationals who have previously obtained an Indian e-Visa or regular/paper visa also have a Visa on Arrival option (60 days, double entry) at 6 designated airports.
Eligible for India e-Visa. Listed as 'Vatican City-Holy See' on the official Indian government e-visa page.

visa_policy.notes: Pakistan nationals and those of Pakistani origin (including those whose parents or grandparents were born in or were permanent residents of Pakistan) are excluded from e-Visa and Visa on Arrival. Diplomatic/Official passport holders are excluded from the Visa on Arrival scheme. All foreign visitors must complete an e-Arrival Card online before arrival via indianvisaonline.gov.in/earrival/. The e-Visa program covers 174 countries per the live official list as of June 2026 (verified via direct WebFetch of indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html). India has no ETA system distinct from its e-Visa; the e-Visa IS the electronic authorization.

visa_types.name: e-Tourist Visa (eTV)
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, short-term recreation, casual visit to meet friends/family
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of 156+ countries. Fee varies by nationality (USD 10, USD 25, or USD 80). Double-entry within 1-year validity. 30-day max stay per entry. 30-day eTV also available. Cannot be extended or converted in India. Only for approved airports and seaports.

visa_types.name: Paper Tourist Visa (Type T)
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, family/friend visits, yoga, short courses
visa_types.notes: Issued by Indian embassies/high commissions/consulates for stays up to 6 months. Multiple-entry 6-month visa most common. Longer durations (1/5/10 years) available for select nationalities. Requires in-person appointment or drop-off at VFS/BLS center.

visa_types.name: e-Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Business meetings, trade fairs, establishing industrial/business ventures, purchase/sale of goods
visa_types.notes: Available for eligible nationalities. Multiple-entry, up to 180 days per visit within 1-year validity. For business activities only; paid employment in India is not permitted.

visa_types.name: e-Medical Visa
visa_types.purpose: Medical treatment at recognized Indian hospitals
visa_types.notes: Triple-entry visa valid 60 days from date of arrival. Requires letter from a recognized Indian hospital confirming treatment. Up to 2 attendants may apply for e-Medical Attendant Visa simultaneously. Available to nationals of eligible countries.

visa_types.name: Student Visa (Type S)
visa_types.purpose: Full-time study at Indian educational institutions
visa_types.notes: Duration of course + 1 month typically. Issued for the period of the course. Requires bonafide admission letter from a recognized Indian institution. Applied at Indian embassy/consulate or VFS/BLS center.

visa_types.name: Employment Visa (Type E)
visa_types.notes: Issued for the term of the employment contract (max 5 years). Requires employer invitation/appointment letter. Salary must meet minimum threshold (generally USD 25,000/year). Applicant must not be taking up a job that can be filled by an Indian national. Applied at Indian embassy/consulate.

conditional_access.conditions: OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card grants a lifelong multiple-entry visa for entry into India for any purpose. No stay limit. Exempt from FRRO/FRO registration for any length of stay. As of the Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026 (effective May 1, 2026, Gazette of India), the OCI credential is now issued digitally as an 'e-OCI' (QR-coded certificate); physical cards remain available on request but are no longer required for travel or immigration clearance. OCI cardholders must obtain a Digital e-Arrival Card before boarding flights to India. OCI card (or e-OCI) must be carried along with the foreign passport. Eligible to persons who were citizens of India at any time after 26 January 1950, or who were eligible to become citizens of India as on 26 January 1950, or who belonged to a territory that became part of India after 15 August 1947, and their children, grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and spouses of Indian citizens or OCI cardholders (marriage subsisting for at least two years). Not available to citizens of Pakistan or Bangladesh. Special/restricted area permits still required for certain zones (e.g. parts of Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh).

credential.subtype: prior Indian e-Visa or regular/paper visa

conditional_access.conditions: Visa-on-Arrival is available only to UAE nationals who had previously obtained an Indian e-Visa or regular/paper sticker visa for India. Double-entry. Permitted purposes: Business, Tourism, Conference, Medical. Non-extendable and non-convertible. Available at 6 airports: Bengaluru, Chennai, Delhi, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Mumbai. Not available if applicant or parents/grandparents were born in or were permanent residents of Pakistan. Not available to holders of Diplomatic/Official passports. Fee: INR 2,000 or equivalent in foreign currency. First-time UAE visitors to India must apply for an e-Visa instead.

India has operational bilateral visa exemption agreements with 107+ countries for holders of diplomatic and/or official/service passports. Most agreements grant 90 days visa-free; some grant shorter stays by agreement. Stay periods and covered passport categories (diplomatic only vs. diplomatic+official vs. diplomatic+official+service) vary by agreement. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered by these agreements. Full list at mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport and mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport-only.htm.

cbi.notes: India does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment program. India prohibits dual citizenship under the Citizenship Act, which makes a conventional CBI program incompatible with Indian law. The Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) scheme provides a long-term multi-entry visa with special privileges but does not confer citizenship. The Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026 introduced a fully digital e-OCI system but did not create a CBI pathway.

programs.name: Permanent Residency Status (PRS) for Foreign Investors
programs.notes: Two investment thresholds: (1) INR 10 crore (INR 10,000,000) within 18 months via FDI route, OR (2) INR 25 crore (INR 25,000,000) within 36 months via FDI route. Both require generating employment for at least 20 resident Indian nationals every financial year. PRS is granted for 10 years with multiple entry and no stay stipulation. Renewable for another 10 years if holder has not come to adverse notice. PRS holders are exempt from FRRO registration. Spouses and dependents receive co-terminus B-1X Visas. PRS holders may purchase one residential property for personal use. No path to citizenship; India does not allow dual citizenship. Not available to Pakistani nationals or third-country nationals of Pakistani origin. Scheme approved by Cabinet on August 31, 2016; instructions issued by MHA on October 17, 2016. Official MHA circular: https://www.mha.gov.in/PDF_Other/prsforeigninvestors_17042017.pdf

programs.name: Fast Track Immigration – Trusted Traveller Programme (FTI-TTP)
programs.eligibility: Indian Nationals and Foreign Nationals holding OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) Card. Minimum age 7 years. Passport valid for at least 6 months. ECR (Emigration Check Required) passport holders are ineligible.
programs.processing_time: Approximately one month after biometric enrollment
programs.notes: Provides expedited immigration clearance at 13 Indian international airports via dedicated e-gates using biometric technology. No enrollment fee (gratis service). Registration valid for 10 years or until passport expiration, whichever is earlier. Airports with e-Gates: Delhi, Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Kolkata, Chennai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Cochin, Lucknow, Amritsar, Calicut, Trivandrum, Trichy. Biometric enrollment available at 31 international airports. Under the Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026, new OCI applicants must sign a biometric consent form to opt into FTI-TTP during OCI registration.

agreements: SAARC Visa Exemption Scheme (limited categories: dignitaries, lawmakers, senior officials, business leaders, journalists, sportspersons - not general public; SVES suspended for Pakistani nationals as of April 2025)

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Nepal and Bhutan visa_free notes updated to include BOI-confirmed rule that citizens must carry a valid passport (plus visa, in Bhutan's case) when entering India from China, Macau, Hong Kong, or Pakistan. Prior record omitted this restriction.
CORRECTED: Maldives notes simplified - removed the unverified '6-month rolling' clause; BOI page states stay must not exceed 90 days without specifying a rolling-period formula.
CORRECTED: 'Cameroon' nationality label corrected to 'Cameroon Union Republic' to match the exact name on the official e-visa eligible countries list.
CORRECTED: 'Vatican City' nationality label corrected to 'Vatican City-Holy See' to match the exact name on the official e-visa eligible countries list.

CORRECTED: UAE entry in e_visa notes updated to remove erroneous reference to 60-day max_stay_days; standard e-Visa max_stay_days is 180 days. The 60-day limit applies only to the Visa on Arrival, which is captured in conditional_access.
CORRECTED: UAE max_stay_days in e_visa changed from 60 to 180 (VoA 60-day limit does not apply to e-Visa path).

CORRECTED: USA e_visa notes simplified - removed the unverified claim about '10-year Business Visa for US citizens' on the e-visa route; 10-year B visa is a separate regular visa category and cannot be confirmed from the e-visa page.
CONFIRMED: e-visa list confirmed at 174 countries via live WebFetch of indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html (June 2026).
CONFIRMED: Visa on Arrival eligibility for Japan, South Korea, and UAE (with prior Indian visa) confirmed via live WebFetch of indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/visa-on-arrival.html.
CONFIRMED: Visa on Arrival available at 6 airports (Bengaluru, Chennai, Delhi, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Mumbai), 60 days, double entry, fee INR 2,000, not for diplomatic/official passports.

UPDATED: OCI conditional_access entry updated to reflect Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026 (effective May 1, 2026, Gazette of India): OCI credential now issued digitally as e-OCI; physical card no longer mandatory for travel; Digital e-Arrival Card required before boarding.
UPDATED: FTI-TTP notes updated to reflect Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026 requirement for new OCI applicants to consent to biometric enrollment during registration.
UPDATED: agreements entry updated to note SVES suspension for Pakistani nationals as of April 2025.

UPDATED: visa_policy.notes updated to remove unverified 72-hour e-Arrival Card window claim (official page just says 'before arrival'); removed '165-174' estimate and replaced with confirmed 174 countries.
SOURCE CHANGE: boi.gov.in visa_free source_url updated from the now-404 path to the confirmed alternate BOI page path for the visa-free regime content.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Full re-fetch of indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html confirmed 174 e-visa eligible nationalities - no additions or removals detected versus current record. All 174 entries in visa_policy.e_visa match the live official page.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/visa-on-arrival.html re-fetched and confirmed - Visa on Arrival remains limited to Japan, South Korea, and UAE (prior India visa/e-visa holders only); fee INR 2,000, 60 days double-entry, 6 airports. No changes detected.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: visa_free entries for Nepal, Bhutan, and Maldives - conditions consistent with BOI WebSearch snippets (BOI direct URLs remain HTTP 404). No contradicting evidence found; entries preserved as-is per safety rule.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: No new visa-free regime introductions for ordinary passport holders found in official sources. Pakistan-origin exclusion from e-Visa and VoA still in force per live official page.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Some third-party sources cite 175 e-visa eligible countries (Ministry of Tourism, March 2026 claim); however, the live official indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html page as of June 2026 lists 174 - the official page count is used.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Full re-fetch of indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html confirms 174 e-visa eligible nationalities. Kenya appears twice in the raw portal HTML (a rendering artifact) but is correctly recorded once in this file. All 174 e_visa entries in visa_policy.e_visa match the live official page - no additions or removals detected.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/visa-on-arrival.html re-fetched and confirmed - Visa on Arrival remains limited to Japan, South Korea, and UAE (UAE requires prior Indian e-Visa or regular/paper visa); fee INR 2,000, 60 days double-entry, 6 airports (Bangalore, Chennai, Delhi, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Mumbai). No changes detected.

INVESTIGATED 2026-06-29: Third-party sources (e.g. india-online-visa.com, visahq.com) claim Saudi Arabia was added to India's e-visa list in January 2026. However, the live official portal (indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html) as of June 2026 does NOT list Saudi Arabia among eligible nationalities. Per safety rules (false visa-free is worst error), Saudi Arabia has NOT been added to the e_visa list. This claim is noted in gaps.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Visa-free entries for Nepal, Bhutan, Maldives - boi.gov.in source URL continues to return HTTP 404. No contradicting evidence found from any source. Entries preserved per safety rule (preservation beats guessing when source is unreachable).

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: No new visa-free or visa-on-arrival regimes for ordinary passport holders found from any official Indian government source. Pakistan-origin exclusion from e-Visa and VoA still in force per live official portal.
gaps: The official MHA PRS circular PDF (mha.gov.in/PDF_Other/prsforeigninvestors_17042017.pdf) returns HTTP 403; PRS details sourced from indembassyseoul.gov.in (official Indian embassy domain). Direct verification of INR thresholds from the MHA circular was not possible.

The boi.gov.in visa-free pages return HTTP 404 on all attempted direct fetches (content/pages/572eefaa-f918-4f64-af0a-a3a4f2157b0f and multiple alternate BOI content paths); details for Nepal/Bhutan/Maldives visa-free regime sourced from BOI WebSearch result snippets and confirmed consistent with record. BOI main site also returns 404 as of June 2026.

The mha.gov.in OCI cardholder page returns HTTP 403, preventing direct page verification. OCI rights confirmed via multiple official search result snippets and Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026 news from official government news sources (newsonair.gov.in).

The complete per-country detail of India's bilateral diplomatic/official passport visa exemption agreements (107+ countries) is accessible at mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport; the conditional_access passport_type entry summarises the overall picture without per-country breakdowns.

India does NOT grant visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to ordinary passport holders based on holding a foreign third-country credential (US visa, Schengen, UK visa, Canadian PR, etc.); no such rule exists in Indian official sources and none has been recorded.
No startup visa or digital nomad visa scheme found on official Indian government sources as of June 2026.

The e-visa eligible country count is confirmed at 174 per the live official page; the record captures all 174 nationalities listed on the page as of June 2026. A March 2026 Ministry of Tourism communication cited 175 countries but the live portal shows 174 - portal is authoritative.

Protected area permits (PAP/RAP) are still required even for OCI cardholders in restricted zones (e.g. parts of Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh); this is noted in the OCI conditions but full list of restricted areas is not enumerated.

Saudi Arabia e-visa eligibility: Multiple third-party sources (india-online-visa.com, visahq.com dated Jan 2026) claim Saudi Arabia was added to India's e-visa list in January 2026, but the live official portal (indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html) as of June 2026 does not list Saudi Arabia. Saudi Arabia has NOT been added to this record until confirmed from the official portal. Requires monitoring at each future validation.

outbound_official.notes: Source: Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India - 'Visa Facility for Indian Nationals (Ordinary Passports)' (mea.gov.in/VFFIN.htm), last updated 26 May 2026, data current as of 2 February 2026. Malaysia visa-free valid until 31 December 2026. Myanmar visa-on-arrival valid up to 29 August 2026. Philippines e-visa in force from 28 October 2024. Mauritania e-visa from 5 January 2025. Kenya moved from e-visa to ETA from 5 January 2024. Some destinations appear in multiple categories (both visa-free and e-visa, or both visa-on-arrival and e-visa) as the MEA page lists them under more than one regime. Citizens are advised to verify with the destination country's mission before travel.
